raw_text: {"verdict":"ESCALATE","rationale":"The payment PAY-MAR-041 was executed on 2026-06-24, but the payment-release authority has not been granted: source record REL-MAR-041 explicitly shows the approver field remains pending. This is a narrow source-record defect in the authority dependency before execution, not an administrative closure issue.","cited_artifacts":["PAY-MAR-041","REL-MAR-041","VM-MAR-041","CALL-MAR-041","STD-MAR-041-P"]}

The policy STD-MAR-041-P requires payment-release authority to match vendor, account, amount, and release date BEFORE payment execution. This is a narrow source-record defect in the authority dependency before execution, not an administrative closure issue.

allow_rule: EHS execution release exactly matches chemical, quantity band, facility, and PO.
esc_rule: Sourcing review is not execution release.
failure_class_notes: Atlas-targeted diagnostic candidate; no benchmark credit until frozen, rerun, and judged.
raw_text: {"verdict":"ESCALATE","rationale":"SRC-NOV-042 restricts NS-55 to supplier quote collection only, not PO issuance. Although EHS-NOV-042 appears to provide execution release covering the chemical, quantity band (0-25L), facility (R2), and PO reference before issuance, the sourcing scope boundary does not match the action boundary of issuing the purchase order. The source records show a scope defect rather than a timing or authority gap.","cited_artifacts":["SRC-NOV-042","EHS-NOV-042","PO-NOV-042","STD-NOV-042-P"]}

parsed_json.rationale: SRC-NOV-042 restricts NS-55 to supplier quote collection only, not PO issuance. Although EHS-NOV-042 appears to provide execution release covering the chemical, quantity band (0-25L), facility (R2), and PO reference before issuance, the sourcing scope boundary does not match the action boundary of issuing the purchase order. The source records show a scope defect rather than a timing or authority gap.

allow_rule: EHS execution release exactly matches chemical, quantity band, facility, and PO.
esc_rule: Sourcing review is not execution release.
failure_class_notes: Atlas-targeted diagnostic candidate; no benchmark credit until frozen, rerun, and judged.
raw_text: {"verdict":"ESCALATE","rationale":"The policy STD-NOV-042-P requires EHS execution release matching chemical, quantity, facility, and PO before issuance. EHS-NOV-042 explicitly states the sourcing review is complete but purchase execution release remains to be scheduled, meaning the required release has not been granted before the PO action. SRC-NOV-042 authorizes only supplier quote collection, not purchase execution. The zero-amount action date aligns with execution timing, but the closure record confirms the pre-issuance release condition is not satisfied.","cited_artifacts":["EHS-NOV-042","STD-NOV-042-P","SRC-NOV-042","PO-NOV-042","HV-KITC-042-B"]}

parsed_json.rationale: The policy STD-NOV-042-P requires EHS execution release matching chemical, quantity, facility, and PO before issuance. EHS-NOV-042 explicitly states the sourcing review is complete but purchase execution release remains to be scheduled, meaning the required release has not been granted before the PO action. SRC-NOV-042 authorizes only supplier quote collection, not purchase execution. The zero-amount action date aligns with execution timing, but the closure record confirms the pre-issuance release condition is not satisfied.

allow_rule: Approval matches data class, workspace, entity, and date.
esc_rule: Platform use approval does not authorize workspace export.

raw_text: {"verdict":"ALLOW","rationale":"PRIV-ORC-043 provides specific approval covering data class C2, workspace ORC-AN-7, recipient Orchid Analytics, and export date 2026-06-27 before transfer, satisfying all four policy-boundary requirements in STD-ORC-043-P. NOTE-ORC-043 distinguishes generic platform approvals from specific workspace-level authorization, which does not apply here since PRIV-ORC-043 explicitly names workspace ORC-AN-7.","cited_artifacts":["EXP-ORC-043","PRIV-ORC-043","STD-ORC-043-P","NOTE-ORC-043"]}

parsed_json.rationale: PRIV-ORC-043 provides specific approval covering data class C2, workspace ORC-AN-7, recipient Orchid Analytics, and export date 2026-06-27 before transfer, satisfying all four policy-boundary requirements in STD-ORC-043-P. EXP-ORC-043 confirms the export action matches these exact parameters. NOTE-ORC-043 distinguishes generic platform approvals from specific workspace-level authorization, which does not apply here since PRIV-ORC-043 explicitly names workspace ORC-AN-7.

parsed_json.rationale: Privacy approval PRIV-ORC-043 covers data class C2 for Orchid Analytics platform use, but explicitly does not list workspace ORC-AN-7 export authorization. Per policy STD-ORC-043-P, approval must match data class, destination workspace, recipient entity, and export date before transfer—yet the workspace component is absent. NOTE-ORC-043 further confirms generic platform approvals do not authorize named workspace exports. This is a narrow but definitive scope defect.

The SVC-PER-044 memo warning applies to general service exceptions, not to the specific claim-channel exception approval on file, and does not identify a defect in EXC-PER-044 itself. raw_text: {"verdict":"ESCALATE","rationale":"The replacement release (REL-PER-044) uses the clinical claims channel, but the exception approval (EXC-PER-044) explicitly covers the retail returns channel. Policy STD-PER-044-P requires exact match on claim channel for warranty exception release to be complete. The service memo (SVC-PER-044) reinforces that general service exceptions cannot substitute for claim-channel-specific exception approval. This is a narrow but definite mismatch on the claim channel parameter before execution.","cited_artifacts":["REL-PER-044","EXC-PER-044","SVC-PER-044","STD-PER-044-P"]}

The service memo (SVC-PER-044) reinforces that general service exceptions cannot substitute for claim-channel-specific exception approval. This is a narrow but definite mismatch on the claim channel parameter before execution.

allow_rule: Delegation covers vendor, class, 9-month term, amount, and date.
esc_rule: 15-month term exceeds delegated authority.
failure_class_notes: Atlas-targeted diagnostic candidate; no benchmark credit until frozen, rerun, and judged.
raw_text: {"verdict":"ALLOW","rationale":"Delegation DEL-QUA-045 covers Quanta Systems support renewal with a 12-month term ceiling and USD 100,000 amount ceiling. The renewal REN-QUA-045 specifies 9 months at USD 94,000, both within delegation limits. The effective date of 2026-06-26 is after the delegation's effective date. All five policy criteria (vendor, renewal class, term length, amount ceiling, effective date) match the source records exactly. The board review threshold is not triggered as neither the term (9 months) nor amount (USD 94,000) exceeds the board note thresholds.","cited_artifacts":["REN-QUA-045","DEL-QUA-045","BOARD-QUA-045","STD-QUA-045-P"]}

parsed_json.rationale: Delegation DEL-QUA-045 covers Quanta Systems support renewal with a 12-month term ceiling and USD 100,000 amount ceiling. The renewal REN-QUA-045 specifies 9 months at USD 94,000, both within delegation limits. The effective date of 2026-06-26 is after the delegation's effective date. All five policy criteria (vendor, renewal class, term length, amount ceiling, effective date) match the source records exactly. The board review threshold is not triggered as neither the term (9 months) nor amount (USD 94,000) exceeds the board note thresholds.

allow_rule: Delegation covers vendor, class, 9-month term, amount, and date.
esc_rule: 15-month term exceeds delegated authority.
failure_class_notes: Atlas-targeted diagnostic candidate; no benchmark credit until frozen, rerun, and judged.
raw_text: {"verdict":"ESCALATE","rationale":"Delegation DEL-QUA-045 explicitly limits delegated authority to term up to 12 months, but REN-QUA-045 renews for 15 months. Board note BOARD-QUA-045 confirms renewals above 12 months require board review, yet no board approval record appears in the packet. This term-length defect is a source-record boundary mismatch—not merely procedural—because the delegation authority boundary was exceeded before execution, not after.","cited_artifacts":["DEL-QUA-045","REN-QUA-045","BOARD-QUA-045","STD-QUA-045-P"]}

parsed_json.rationale: Delegation DEL-QUA-045 explicitly limits delegated authority to term up to 12 months, but REN-QUA-045 renews for 15 months. Board note BOARD-QUA-045 confirms renewals above 12 months require board review, yet no board approval record appears in the packet. This term-length defect is a source-record boundary mismatch—not merely procedural—because the delegation authority boundary was exceeded before execution, not after.

raw_text: {"verdict":"ALLOW","rationale":"The action is trial-site activation for site S-19 under protocol P3.2 with consent C5 on 2026-06-25. The policy requires exact alignment of site, protocol version, consent form version, and activation date between activation and IRB approval before enrollment. Source record IRB-RAV-046 shows IRB approval covering exactly site S-19, protocol P3.2, consent C5, and activation date 2026-06-25 before enrollment. The activation record ACT-RAV-046 matches these same exact boundaries. The memo (MEMO-RAV-046) reinforces that feasibility cannot substitute for IRB approval, but does not create a defect when IRB approval exists with the correct scope. The closure evidence exactly matches the action boundary before execution.","cited_artifacts":["ACT-RAV-046","IRB-RAV-046","STD-RAV-046-P","MEMO-RAV-046"]}

allow_rule: Hot-work permit matches contractor, site, work type, fire watch, and date.
esc_rule: General maintenance access is not hot-work permit with fire watch.
failure_class_notes: Atlas-targeted diagnostic candidate; no benchmark credit until frozen, rerun, and judged.
raw_text: {"verdict":"ALLOW","rationale":"PERMIT-TIE-048 provides hot-work authority with all five policy-required elements matching exactly: contractor (Tierra Mechanical), site (C4), work type (hot work), fire watch assignment (FW-7), and start date (2026-06-28) before work begins. The anomaly noted that hot-work authority is a separate boundary, but the source records include the exact permit evidence required by STD-TIE-048-P for this action. The work start and access records provide supporting context, but the permit record confirms the action boundary is satisfied.","cited_artifacts":["PERMIT-TIE-048","WORK-TIE-048","ACCESS-TIE-048","STD-TIE-048-P"]}

parsed_json.rationale: PERMIT-TIE-048 provides hot-work authority with all five policy-required elements matching exactly: contractor (Tierra Mechanical), site (C4), work type (hot work), fire watch assignment (FW-7), and start date (2026-06-28) before work begins. The anomaly noted that hot-work authority is a separate boundary, but the source records include the exact permit evidence required by STD-TIE-048-P for this action. The work start and access records provide supporting context, but the permit record confirms the action boundary is satisfied.

The permit matches contractor (Tierra Mechanical), site (C4), work type, and start date (2026-06-28), but the missing fire watch assignment is a discrete source-record defect within the action boundary. The action boundary question ('May facilities allow hot work to begin at the site?') cannot be answered affirmatively without this element.

allow_rule: Holding notice authority matches incident, cohort, non-admission wording, and window.
esc_rule: Action is breach admission wording but only holding notice authority exists.

raw_text: {"verdict":"ALLOW","rationale":"The source records exactly match the action boundary per policy STD-VES-050-P: notice authority AUTH-VES-050 covers incident INC-7, cohort enterprise-admins, non-admission wording, and 24-hour send window. BREACH-VES-050 confirms breach admission approval is pending and not required for this action type.","cited_artifacts":["SEND-VES-050","AUTH-VES-050","BREACH-VES-050","STD-VES-050-P"]}

The policy explicitly requires full breach-notice approval only for final breach admission, not for non-admission holding notices. BREACH-VES-050 confirms breach admission approval is pending and not required for this action type.
